zotero.abstractNote: We propose a novel framework for learning transferable meta-representations by leveraging the functional structure of tasks. Our approach, Function Contrastive Learning (FCL), learns representations that are invariant to task-specific transformations while being sensitive to the underlying functional relationships between tasks. We demonstrate that FCL achieves state-of-the-art performance on several meta-learning benchmarks, showing improved transferability and robustness compared to existing methods.
